title: D1/D2/D3: the session preamble stops printing the retired model — and D9's four derived artifacts
action: The preamble is what every agent reads before doing anything, and two of its sections currently teach the model f3 retired. BOTH gaps were confirmed by direct observation of a live session preamble, not inferred. (D1) `skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/inject.mjs:44` `criticalPatternsDigest()` reads `docs/history/learnings/critical-patterns.md` and injects its first 10 non-blank lines under `### Critical patterns (digest)` (call site :327-331). That file is now a POINTER STUB, so every session receives the stub's redirect boilerplate as its 'digest' — dead weight where the lessons should be. Make it resolve through `bundleMode`: in bundle mode build the digest from the bundle's own generated root index `docs/knowledge/index.md`, its `## Critical patterns` section (the live equivalent D21/D34 established — read the stub at docs/history/learnings/critical-patterns.md to confirm the pointer before you code against it); with NO bundle, behavior byte-identical to today. Same line cap in both branches. (D2) `projectMapLines()` (:68-106) always reports `- Specced areas: N (docs/specs/ — read the spec before the code)` off a flat `docs/specs/*.md` count, with no bundle branch — it counts the compatibility surface and instructs the reader to do what G4 replaced. Branch it on `bundleMode`: bundle mode names the BUNDLE as the thing to read before the code and counts what the bundle actually holds (areas and concepts), and describes `docs/specs/` as the read-only compatibility surface if it names it at all; no bundle = byte-identical to today INCLUDING the missing-map warning branch. The PBI line rides BOTH branches as it does today, and the section's 2-5 line cap (preamble contract D5/D10) holds in both. (D3) the scribing-debt nudge at :303 hardcodes `settled behavior belongs in docs/specs/` — make it name the resolved target. Both doc trees resolve through `resolveProductRoot` as they already do here (:69-71) — never join them yourself (G13). TESTS: `skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_misc.mjs:290,311` pin `Specced areas: N`; update them and ADD bundle-mode coverage for all three changes, including a no-bundle fixture proving byte-identical legacy behavior (the fallback is untestable in this repo, which HAS a bundle — use the fixture discipline test_bundle_mode.mjs already established). RED-FIRST: capture the current preamble output showing the stub boilerplate and the `Specced areas` line BEFORE changing anything, and quote it in the evidence. D9 — THIS CELL OWNS THE DERIVED-ARTIFACT REGEN FOR THE WHOLE FEATURE, after f4-1 and f4-2 have landed: (1) copy every touched `templates/lib/*.mjs` to its `.bee/bin/lib/` sibling, (2) `node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs`, (3) `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write`, (4) `node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply` to refresh the managed-hash ledger. Then run the FULL chain as the LAST action — a cap-time verify expires the moment anything else is edited (pattern 20260715, fourth recurrence, and the exact failure that left the previous session red).

truths: node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 — run as the LAST action, after every edit and all four regens

red evidence: the CURRENT preamble output quoted, showing both the pointer-stub boilerplate under '### Critical patterns (digest)' and the 'Specced areas: N (docs/specs/ — read the spec before the code)' line
green evidence: the NEW preamble output quoted, showing the digest sourced from the bundle and the project map naming the bundle
a no-bundle fixture proves the legacy branch of ALL THREE changes is byte-identical to today
test_lib_mirror, release_manifest --check, test_plugin_distribution and ledger_parity --check are all green (the four derived artifacts, D9)
prohibitions: never decide bundle mode by existsSync or by re-deriving the rule in prose — bundleMode is the only answer (G12)
never join docs/knowledge or docs/specs off a bare root — resolveProductRoot, as this file already does (G13)
do not exceed the preamble project-map section's 2-5 line cap in either branch
do not delete or rewrite the critical-patterns pointer stub (D20)
do not weaken or delete the existing test_misc assertions — update them and ADD coverage

trace.outcome: D1/D2/D3 closed: the session preamble stops teaching the retired model. The critical-patterns digest was handing every session four lines of YAML and six of forwarding address from a pointer stub; it now routes on the one bundle predicate and, in bundle mode, states the total (49) and lists the nine most recent real lessons newest-first from the bundle's generated index — newest-first because those rows are date-ordered and a naive first-N cut would surface the ten OLDEST forever. The project map counted the read-only compatibility surface as 'Specced areas: 11' and told every session to read the spec before the code; it now names the bundle and counts what the bundle holds. The scribing-debt nudge names the resolved target instead of hardcoding docs/specs. The no-bundle branch is proven byte-identical two ways: a permanent bundle-less fixture suite pinning every line as an exact literal, and a direct whole-preamble byte-diff against the pre-change implementation. All four D9 derived artifacts regenerated for the whole feature and green.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "BEFORE, captured by calling buildSessionPreamble() against the live repo (which IS in bundle mode: 11 areas, 122 concepts) and preserved verbatim at docs/history/okf-integration-close-f4/reports/red-preamble-before.md. The '### Critical patterns (digest)' section printed: '---', 'area: critical-patterns', 'updated: 2026-07-22', 'migrated_to: docs/knowledge/patterns/', '---', '# Critical Patterns (migrated — pointer stub)', then the stub's redirect prose. FOUR of ten lines were YAML frontmatter delimiters and keys; the other six were the forwarding address. Not one line was a lesson. The '### Project map' section printed '- Specced areas: 11 (docs/specs/ — read the spec before the code)' — counting the read-only compatibility surface and instructing every session to use the reading order G4 replaced. And the scribing-debt nudge read 'settled behavior belongs in docs/specs/ before it evaporates'. No suite was red for any of it; that is the silent-rot class, which is why the before-state is a captured artifact rather than a stack trace.",
  "green_evidence": "AFTER, same call: Project map now reads '- Knowledge bundle: docs/knowledge/ (index: docs/knowledge/index.md) — read the bundle before the code' and '- Bundle holds: 11 area(s), 122 concept(s) (docs/specs/ is the read-only compatibility surface)'. The digest now reads '- 49 critical pattern(s) in the bundle — the 9 most recent below; full list: docs/knowledge/index.md (\"Critical patterns\")' followed by nine real, dated, linked lessons newest-first. Not one line of the stub boilerplate survives, and the section caps hold.",
  "no_bundle_proof": "TWO independent layers, both green. (1) A permanent fixture suite in test_bundle_mode.mjs (10 new rows, 41 -> 51 passing) pins the bundle-less branch as EXACT LITERALS: the whole Project map section line-for-line, the missing-map warning branch line-for-line, the legacy 10-line digest, and the debt nudge word-for-word ending 'belongs in docs/specs/ before it evaporates (decision 0011).', plus a row asserting the WHOLE preamble never mentions the bundle and never nags. (2) A direct BYTE-DIFF against HEAD: the pre-change inject.mjs was copied into a scratch lib tree and both implementations built the preamble from the identical bundle-less fixture — 'IDENTICAL whole preamble, both maps present (924 bytes)' and 'IDENTICAL whole preamble, missing-map warning branch (858 bytes)'. Full string equality across the entire preamble, not merely the three changed sections. A never-migrated host cannot tell this release happened (G1).",
  "bundle_branch_coverage": "The bundle rows pin: the 2-5 line cap, the ABSENCE of 'Specced areas' and of 'read the spec before the code', the stated total with the N most-recent rows newest-first, and — the trap a naive first-N cut would fall into — that the three OLDEST fixture patterns are ABSENT. Also pinned: section-boundary respect, link rewriting from bundle-relative to repo-root-relative, and a divorced-topology row proving resolveProductRoot is honoured (counts come from the product repo, nothing joined off the bare root, G13).",
  "derived_artifacts_d9": "All four regens run as the last actions, for the WHOLE feature: .bee/bin/lib mirror copy, plugin skill tree render, release manifest write, self-onboard for the managed-hash ledger. The .claude/ and .agents/ trees also picked up f4-1/f4-2's prose, which those cells' commit had not synced — exactly why D9 gave the regen to the last cell. All four gates green: test_lib_mirror, release_manifest --check, test_plugin_distribution, ledger_parity --check.",
  "chain": "env -u BEE_AGENT_NAME node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS 79 suite(s), exit 0. Re-run INDEPENDENTLY by the orchestrator after the worker reported, not accepted on the worker's word; git status showed zero drift, so the green describes the tree as capped.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Full inject.mjs diff read by the orchestrator. Confirmed independently that bundleDir() routes through resolveProductRoot (knowledge.mjs:144-146) and collectConcepts() goes through bundleDir, so the bundle branch is product-root-safe and G13 holds — the worker used the safe helpers rather than joining paths itself. Two judgement calls inside D1 reviewed and accepted: (a) in bundle mode with no generated index or no critical section the digest emits NOTHING rather than falling back to the retired file — falling back would re-print the stub, which is the bug; (b) index links are rewritten to repo-root-relative so a session can open them as printed.",
  "harness_defect_found": "The worker's first chain run came back with 3 write-guard failures, all 'the acting session's own hold must never block its own write'. Cause: the BEE_AGENT_NAME prefix that bee's own swarm rule mandates on write-heavy commands LEAKS into the suites run_verify spawns and becomes the acting agent identity inside checkWrite. Reproduced by the orchestrator: env -u BEE_AGENT_NAME -> 31 passed 0 failed; BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test -> 28 passed 3 failed. This also RETROACTIVELY EXPLAINS the f4-1/f4-2 'transient' red, which the orchestrator had wrongly attributed to concurrent chain runs. Filed as friction; the fix belongs in run_verify's env scrub, which already scrubs CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID and BEE_SESSION_ID and simply missed this third variable."
}
